title: SPARTA Portfolio Capital-Efficiency Protocol Memo v1
lane_history_note: This v1 memo OPENS a NEW research lane that studies how research capital-at-risk could be allocated ACROSS SPARTA's existing strategy bench. It does NOT propose, change, or execute any allocation. It consumes only FROZEN, committed research artifacts (read-only). It never reads live positions, broker balances, or in-flight working-tree state of any candidate.
purpose: Define -- specification only -- how SPARTA will study capital efficiency across its bench of researched strategy candidates: diversification, overlap/redundancy, concentration limits, and capital-at-risk budgeting. The output of the FULL lane (future, separately authorized) is an ADVISORY memo only; no capital is ever allocated, sized, or executed by this lane.

in_scope: Read-only analysis of FROZEN, committed research artifacts (e.g., strategy_report_registry / strategy_candidate_registry outputs, frozen per-strategy backtest report.json verdicts).
Defining capital-efficiency metrics on paper (formulas + inputs named, NOT computed in this bundle).
Defining baseline allocation models on paper (equal-weight, inverse-volatility, capped-weight) as research reference points only.
Defining the shape of a future ADVISORY allocation memo (per-candidate suggested research weight + rationale + caveats).
out_of_scope: Live capital. Real broker/exchange balances. Real positions.
Position sizing for execution. Order sizing. Order placement.
Any change to live or paper trading logic or execution files.
Computing any metric or allocation in this bundle (P0 is specification only).
Reading, modifying, or depending on any in-flight (uncommitted) candidate working-tree state.

c10_boundary.note: This lane MUST NOT read-for-mutation, edit, stage, or depend on the in-flight Candidate #10 (intraweek calendar seasonality) files, tests, labels, replay, detector, or working-tree state. If C10 ever produces a FROZEN, committed verdict artifact, that frozen artifact may later be treated like any other read-only bench input -- but only the committed/frozen form, never the working tree.

input_sources.form: frozen committed report.json artifacts under reports/
input_sources.note: Verdicts are verdict-ceilinged to WATCH by the upstream registry; this lane inherits that ceiling and never surfaces PASS/ACTIVE/STRONG.
Candidate status / evidence_level descriptions only; never live positions.

input_sources.form: committed per-strategy report.json with PASS/WATCH/FAIL verdict + cost-aware stats
input_sources.note: Only datasets that were FROZEN with checksums upstream are admissible; manually edited or uncommitted artifacts are inadmissible.

inadmissible_inputs: Live broker / exchange balances or positions.
Any uncommitted working-tree artifact (including in-flight C10).
Any dataset that was not frozen with checksums upstream.
Any number sourced from a live network fetch.

capital_efficiency_metrics.label: Capital-at-risk budget
capital_efficiency_metrics.summary: A pre-declared total research capital-at-risk envelope that any future advisory allocation must sum within. Defined here; never sized or executed.

capital_efficiency_metrics.label: Candidate overlap / redundancy
capital_efficiency_metrics.summary: Pairwise similarity of two candidates' signal exposure (same asset / same regime / same direction) used to flag redundant capital. Estimation method is defined in a later phase; NOT computed here.

capital_efficiency_metrics.label: Diversification ratio
capital_efficiency_metrics.summary: Ratio of weighted-average standalone risk to combined portfolio risk; higher means capital is spread across less-correlated edges. Defined; never computed in this bundle.

capital_efficiency_metrics.label: Concentration limit
capital_efficiency_metrics.summary: Maximum share of the capital-at-risk budget any single candidate / asset / family may hold in a future advisory allocation.

capital_efficiency_metrics.label: Marginal capital efficiency
capital_efficiency_metrics.summary: Advisory return-per-unit-of-marginal-risk a candidate adds to the bench, used only to RANK candidates for human review -- never to auto-size capital.

allocation_baselines.label: Equal-weight reference
allocation_baselines.summary: Every admissible candidate receives the same research weight. Reference baseline only.

allocation_baselines.label: Inverse-volatility reference
allocation_baselines.summary: Weight inversely proportional to each candidate's frozen standalone risk. Reference baseline only.

allocation_baselines.label: Capped-weight reference
allocation_baselines.summary: Equal- or inverse-vol weighting subject to the concentration_limit cap. Reference baseline only.

allocation_baselines.label: Discretionary human allocation
allocation_baselines.summary: Operator allocates by judgment using the advisory memo. This lane informs, it does not decide.

validation_phases.purpose: This bundle. Lock language, scope, capital boundaries, kill rules. No data, no compute, no allocation.
Define which FROZEN committed artifacts this lane may read + the admissibility/freeze rules. Spec only; no read run.
Pin exact formulas + inputs for each capital-efficiency metric. Spec only; no metric computed.
Pin the equal-weight / inverse-vol / capped-weight reference models + the concentration-limit cap. Spec only; no weights computed.
Pin the overlap / correlation / redundancy estimation method + its data requirements. Spec only; no estimate computed.
Pin the schema of the future ADVISORY allocation memo (per-candidate suggested research weight + rationale + caveats). Spec only.
Define how an advisory allocation's stability is stress-tested against estimation error (correlation drift, expectancy decay). Spec; compute requires separate authorization.
Paper allocation SIMULATION ONLY (no broker, no order, no live capital) -- and ONLY if a SEPARATE future authorization grants this phase.
EXPLICITLY OUT OF SCOPE for this protocol stack. Allocating real capital requires a SEPARATE far-future protocol + authorizations after evidence exists.

pass_watch_fail_rules.PASS: A future ADVISORY allocation is PASS-grade for review only when: it draws solely on FROZEN admissible inputs; it respects the pre-declared capital-at-risk budget and concentration limits; it is stable under the P6 estimation-error stress; it does not concentrate the bench into a single asset / family / regime; every weight carries a written rationale + caveat; and no rule was changed mid-study. PASS means 'fit for human review', NEVER 'execute'.
pass_watch_fail_rules.WATCH: Advisory allocation shows a plausible efficiency improvement but at least one of: high sensitivity to correlation/expectancy estimation error; reliance on a thin set of candidates; concentration near a limit; inputs only partially frozen. Stays advisory; flagged for caution.
pass_watch_fail_rules.FAIL: Allocation depends on inadmissible/unfrozen inputs; breaches the capital-at-risk budget or a concentration limit; collapses under estimation-error stress; or implicitly assumes execution. FAIL allocations are discarded, not down-weighted.

kill_conditions: Any input is sourced from live balances, live positions, a network fetch, or an uncommitted working tree (including in-flight C10).
An advisory allocation is treated, described, or wired as if it were an execution instruction.
A concentration limit or the capital-at-risk budget is silently exceeded.
A metric or weight is computed inside the P0/spec bundles (compute belongs to separately authorized later phases).
Upstream verdict ceiling (WATCH max) is bypassed so PASS/ACTIVE/STRONG leaks into an allocation.
Results cannot be reproduced bit-for-bit from the named frozen inputs by a second offline run.
required_future_artifacts: Portfolio Capital-Efficiency Input Contract v1 (P1) -- which frozen artifacts are admissible + freeze rules. Spec only.
Portfolio Capital-Efficiency Metric Spec v1 (P2) -- exact formulas + inputs. Spec only.
Portfolio Capital-Efficiency Allocation Baseline Spec v1 (P3) -- reference models + concentration cap. Spec only.
Portfolio Capital-Efficiency Overlap/Correlation Method v1 (P4) -- redundancy estimation method. Spec only.
Portfolio Capital-Efficiency Advisory Memo Schema v1 (P5) -- shape of the human-review allocation memo.
Lane closeout memo if the capital-efficiency idea PARKs / RETIREs.
safety_boundaries: No broker control, no exchange connection, no API keys, no .env, no credential handling.
No live trading. No live capital allocation. No paper-order execution. No order placement. No order sizing for execution.
No scheduler / background daemon. No external network calls in this protocol's runtime.
No data fetch in this bundle. No metric computed in this bundle. No allocation computed in this bundle.
Do not modify paper / live execution files. Do not touch Candidate #10 files, tests, labels, replay, detector, or working-tree state.
Consume only FROZEN, committed, admissible artifacts; never live positions or uncommitted working trees.
Do not claim profitability. Do not claim live-readiness. Do not claim STRONG evidence from this protocol alone.
A capital-efficiency score does not imply future returns. An advisory allocation is not an execution instruction. No allocation is authorized by this protocol alone.
no_profit_claim_policy: This protocol does not imply edge and does not allocate capital.
A capital-efficiency score does not imply future returns.
An advisory allocation memo is a human-review aid, never an execution instruction.
No paper or live capital allocation is authorized.
No allocation is authorized by this protocol alone (P5+ outputs require their own separate authorization).
